title: cuscuta ruschanica
intro: Cuscuta ruschanica, also known as Dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and extracting nutrients. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asia and Eastern Europe, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta ruschanica include improving digestion, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of various diseases such as rheumatism and anemia. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta ruschanica is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
